tags.description: Drift dive site with strong currents. Also known as Batu Tiga, requires 50+ dives and Advanced Open Water certification. Diverse scuba diving with sloping reefs, craggy rocks and walls covered in hard and soft corals. Visibility often reaches 30 meters. Strong and unpredictable currents especially during full moon. Living Seas coral restoration projects nearby.
tags.name: Gili Mimpang

name: USAT Liberty Wreck
validation_source: PADI, ScubaBoard, Multiple dive operators, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.description: Historic WWII-era American transport ship (USAT Liberty, sunk 1942). Accessible wreck dive from volcanic black-sand beach shore entry. Wreck top at 4-5m, bottom at 35m. Excellent for beginners and more experienced divers alike. Rich marine life including batfish, lionfish, sweetlips, manta rays, reef sharks and sea turtles. Notable for wreck penetration opportunities and excellent photography.
tags.name: USAT Liberty Wreck

tags.description: Located in Menjangan Island area (northwest Bali). Gentle coral reef site with calm waters and good visibility. Ideal for beginners and training dives. Part of West Bali National Marine Park. Rich marine life with coral formations and reef fish.
tags.name: Coral Garden

tags.description: Cave dive site in Menjangan Island area. Features cave formations and cavern diving opportunities. Wall starts at ~12m depth with gorgonians, sea fans, and sponges. Varied depth 3-40m depending on route. Requires intermediate diving skills.
tags.name: Bat Cave

name: Manta Point
validation_source: PADI, Nusa Penida diving guides, Multiple operators, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.description: Famous manta ray cleaning station on southwest coast of Nusa Penida. Manta cleaning stations at 5-10m depth with maximum depth ~18m. Primary attraction is manta rays visiting to be cleaned by small fish. Over 90% probability of manta encounters March-June. Also hosts blue-spotted stingrays, eagle rays, reef sharks, turtles and occasional mola mola. Shallow, easy water entry makes it accessible to all levels.
tags.name: Manta Point

tags.description: Artificial reef site with underwater shrine. Located in Amed area. Intermediate drift-dive potential with varied depth profile. Cultural and diving attraction.
tags.name: Underwater Shrine

tags.description: Reef dive site in Nusa Penida area. Intermediate-level site with healthy coral structures and diverse marine life. Standard Bali reef diving experience.
tags.name: Bali Scuba

tags.description: Reef dive site near Amed. Intermediate-level reef diving with good visibility and moderate currents. Part of Bali's east coast diving area.
tags.name: Blue Hill Dive Site

tags.description: Reef dive site in Nusa Penida area. Intermediate-level reef diving with healthy coral structures and varied marine life.
tags.name: Water Walk

tags.description: Cave dive site in Menjangan Island area featuring underwater cave and cavern formations. Wall diving with cave features at 12m+ depth. Features gorgonians, sea fans and sponges.
tags.name: Underwater Cave

tags.description: Wall dive site in Menjangan Island area. Coral wall with varied depth and marine life. Part of Menjangan's famous wall-diving region with excellent gorgonian diversity.
tags.name: Blue Corner

tags.description: Reef dive site at jetty location in Menjangan Island. Beginner-friendly with coral formations and calm conditions. Gentle drift possible. Good for training dives.
tags.name: Post I Jetty

tags.description: Reef dive site near mangrove area in Menjangan Island region. Shallow, beginner-friendly diving with coral formations and calm conditions. Excellent for new divers.
tags.name: Mangrove Point

tags.description: One of two Manta Bay dive sites in Nusa Penida. Primary feature is numerous manta rays usually feeding at site. Shallow cleaning station areas (10m) with maximum 20m depth. Listen to experienced boat captains - potential for very strong surges and currents, especially near cliff faces where waves break. Not recommended for beginners in poor conditions, but manageable during calm periods. Best viewed March-June.
tags.description_extended: Site location near cliffs creates wave surge hazard. Rock formations and wall structure. Marine life focused on manta encounters.
tags.name: Manta Bay

tags.description: Shore-entry wall dive site in Amed on Bali's east coast. Gentle sloping reef starting in shallow water, then dropping to colorful reef and wall structure. Excellent site for all experience levels. Abundant macro life including turtles and occasional reef sharks. Usually calm conditions with little current and good visibility. Easily accessed from shore.
tags.name: Amed wall

tags.description: One of Bali's most beginner-friendly and accessible shore dive sites in Padang Bai. Entry from sandy beach, begins on plateau at 5m depth with gentle descent along sandy slope to ~20m. Ideal for Open Water certification courses. Features scattered coral bommies, rocks, table corals with excellent macro photography opportunities. Rich marine life: turtles, reef sharks, nudibranchs, seahorses, scorpionfish, and famous Spanish dancer nudibranch visible on night dives. Can have strong currents despite benign appearance. Mild currents and usually good visibility (15-20m). Underwater statues enhance the site.
tags.name: Blue Lagoon

name: Gili Tepekong North
validation_source: BaliAqua, Gili Tepekong dive guides, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.description: Northern section of Gili Tepekong. Less challenging than southern canyon area. Dive toward north with huge boulders to swim among and open plains of corals. Moderate currents possible but manageable for intermediate divers. Popular for photography due to varied topography and marine life.
tags.name: Gili Tepekong North

tags.description: The Canyon - southern section of Gili Tepekong, known as 'the toilet' due to intimidating swirling downdraft currents. Bali's most challenging and dangerous dive site. Requires Advanced Open Water or equivalent experience (50+ dives minimum). Features big tunnel, walls, and schools of sweetlips, jacks, groupers, white-tip reef sharks. Strong unpredictable currents and surge, especially southwest area. Must follow experienced guide. Best attempted by advanced divers only.
tags.name: Gili Tepekong South

name: Crystal Bay
validation_source: Nusa Penida diving experts, ScubaBoard, Multiple operators, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.description: Penida Bay - famous for mola mola (ocean sunfish) encounters July-October. Also called Penida Bay. Site conditions heavily dependent on currents. Known for strong downcurrents that can force rapid unintended depth increases - serious hazard with multiple documented accidents and fatalities. Requires Advanced Open Water certification minimum. Mola divers typically work 20-40m depth range. Also hosts reef sharks, manta rays, and diverse reef life. Conditions can change rapidly - always use experienced local guides. Best season July-mid November for mola sightings (40-90% encounter rates July-October).
tags.name: Crystal Bay

name: Ped
validation_source: Nusa Penida diving guides, Dive site descriptions

tags.description: Also known as Peed. Drift dive site with wall and reef features. Can be calm when currents absent - excellent for photography. Gentle reef from 0-20m depth, wall dropping from ~20m downward. Requires drift diving experience. Good for macro photography in calm conditions.
tags.name: Ped

tags.description: Reef dive site in Amed area. Intermediate-level site with healthy coral structures, diverse marine life, and good visibility.
tags.name: Batu Belah

tags.description: Reef dive site in Amed area. Intermediate-level site with healthy coral structures and good marine life diversity.
tags.name: Bulakan

tags.description: Distinctive reef dive site in Amed area known for pyramid-shaped rock formations. Intermediate-level diving with interesting topography and coral structures.
tags.name: Pyramids

tags.description: Reef dive site in Tulamben area. Intermediate-level site with healthy coral garden structures supporting diverse marine life.
tags.name: Coral garden

tags.description: Reef dive site near Big Fins TecDiving facility in Tulamben area. Popular for technical and recreational diving. Good coral structures and marine life.
tags.name: Big Fins TecDiving

tags.description: Pos II - gentle drift dive along Menjangan Island's south coast. Wall diving starts at 12m with large gorgonians at depths below 25m. Cold water upwelling brings turtles, manta rays, sharks and occasional mola-mola. Part of Menjangan's famous wall-diving region. Calm conditions and shallow walls make it ideal for beginners despite its 30m maximum depth.
tags.name: Menjangan - Pos 2

tags.description: Reef dive site near Tulamben area. Intermediate-level site with healthy coral structures and good marine life diversity. Part of Bali's popular east coast diving region.
tags.name: Emerald

name: Gili Biaha
validation_source: okdiversbali.com, nicodivescoolbali.com, padi.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Gili Biaha
tags.description: The most remote of the three Amuk Bay islands east of Padangbai. Features a dramatic black wall with a signature Shark Cave at around 10m where white-tip reef sharks rest. Strong and unpredictable currents including downcurrents make this an advanced site. Seasonal mola mola sightings June through October. Rich marine life includes lobsters, nudibranchs, turtles, barracuda, and schools of glass fish. Depth range 6-40m with best visibility June to October.

name: Drop-off
validation_source: aquamarinediving.com, ecodivebali.com, letsdivetulamben.com, ScubaBoard user Dan (GPS)

tags.name: Drop-off
tags.description: Dramatic volcanic wall formed by a historic Mount Agung lava flow, also known as the Tulamben Wall. Shore entry across pebble beach with a short surface swim to the wall. Wall top at 3-5m drops to over 60m. A shallow aquarium section at 5m teems with reef fish. The wall is covered in sponges, gorgonian fans, black coral, and crinoids. Notable marine life includes white-tip reef sharks, Napoleon wrasse, frogfish, ghost pipefish, and nudibranchs. Adjacent to the USAT Liberty wreck.
